ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product class; no specific tandoori-marinated variant exists. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific tandoori chicken wings entry; A00LC (cooked chicken meat) is the nearest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for tandoori chicken wings; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of tandoori chicken wings consists of approximately 4 to 5 wings, weighing around 200 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken wing, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05072)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are based on USDA SR Legacy data for roasted chicken wings (skin on). Tandoori marination with yogurt and spices adds modest protein and sodium; values have been adjusted upward for sodium (~+80 mg/100 g) and marginally for protein (~+1 g/100 g) to reflect the yogurt-and-spice marinade. Energy, fat, and carbohydrate are primarily from the chicken wing itself. This is a medium-confidence adaptation; no exact tandoori chicken wing entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN for this Turkish-style preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tandoori chicken wings. GI is estimated at 15 based on analogous high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled/roasted chicken preparations reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Protein-dominant, low-carbohydrate foods consistently yield GI values in the 10–20 range. GL per 200 g serving = (15 × 2.5 × 2.0) / 100 = 0.75, rounded to 0.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from marinade and spice blend
hypertension.guidance: Each 200 g serving provides approximately 760 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and request reduced-salt marinade preparation to stay within the 2,000 mg/day sodium target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 2.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g and an estimated GI of 15, tandoori chicken wings have a negligible glycemic load and are generally suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided the marinade does not contain added sugars.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from skin-on chicken wings
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Skin-on chicken wings contribute saturated fat (~4 g/100 g) and cholesterol (~85 mg/100 g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should consider removing the skin and moderating portion size to limit saturated fat intake below 7% of total daily energy.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tandoori chicken wings are chicken wings marinated in a spiced yogurt mixture — typically containing cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili powder, garlic, ginger, and lemon juice — and then cooked at high heat in a tandoor clay oven or on a grill. The cooking method produces a characteristic charred exterior, smoky aroma, and juicy interior. The dish originates from the Indian subcontinent but has been widely adopted in Turkish and Middle Eastern cuisines, where it appears on menus alongside kebabs and shawarma. In Singapore, it is served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants such as Sultan Palace. Nutritionally, tandoori chicken wings are high in protein (~21.5 g/100 g), moderate in fat (~14.5 g/100 g, primarily from the skin), and very low in carbohydrates (~2.5 g/100 g), making them suitable for low-carbohydrate dietary patterns. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 15 (low), and the glycemic load per serving is negligible. Sodium content is moderate to high due to the spiced marinade (~380 mg/100 g), which is a consideration for individuals managing hypertension. The dish is naturally gluten-free and halal-certified in most Turkish restaurant contexts. It is typically served as a starter or sharing plate.
common_user_queries: How many calories in tandoori chicken wings?
Are tandoori chicken wings good for diabetics?
Is tandoori chicken wings high in sodium?
Can I eat tandoori chicken wings on a low-carb diet?
What spices are used in tandoori chicken wings?
Tandoori wings vs regular fried wings calories

confidence_reason: No exact tandoori chicken wings entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy roasted chicken wing data (NDB 05072 / FDC 171477) with adjustments for the yogurt-and-spice marinade. GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables for comparable high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled poultry preparations. Medium confidence is appropriate for an adapted estimate from a credible secondary source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA SR Legacy roasted chicken wing (skin on) with upward sodium adjustment (~+80 mg/100 g) and minor protein adjustment (~+1 g/100 g) to reflect yogurt-and-spice marinade contribution. 2. GI estimated at 15 based on analogous grilled/roasted chicken preparations from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021; no direct GI measurement for tandoori chicken wings was located. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, B-vitamins, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for roasted chicken wing; these are not -1 sentinel values but positive estimates from the adapted source. 4. The dish is listed under Turkish cuisine in the field survey data source but tandoori preparation is of Indian subcontinent origin; both cultural contexts are reflected in the description and aliases. 5. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant context (Sultan Palace Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine); this should be verified against the restaurant's halal certification. 6. Serving size of 200 g (approximately 4–5 wings) is a realistic restaurant portion estimate; actual portion weight may vary by restaurant.
